title: Neurology referral documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports focal neurological and hearing red flags, acute vestibular syndrome, HINTS by trained clinicians, neuroimaging referral, and Hallpike assessment for positional vertigo.
red_flags.rationale: Supports selected adult dizziness and vertigo referral features for neurology referral documentation; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports focal neurological and hearing red flags, acute vestibular syndrome, HINTS by trained clinicians, neuroimaging referral, and Hallpike assessment for positional vertigo.

source.title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.7 — dizziness and vertigo in adults

red_flags.text: Supports sudden, progressive, fluctuating, unilateral, symmetrical, sensory-associated, and back-pain-associated weakness, urgent referral, and clinician-led imaging where specified.
red_flags.rationale: Supports selected adult weakness referral features for neurology referral documentation; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports sudden, progressive, fluctuating, unilateral, symmetrical, sensory-associated, and back-pain-associated weakness, urgent referral, and clinician-led imaging where specified.

source.title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.7.1–1.7.10 and 1.10.1–1.10.6 — limb weakness and sensory symptoms

examination.text: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.
examination.rationale: Supports selected adult sensory referral features for neurology referral documentation; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.

source.title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.10.1–1.10.13 — sensory symptoms including tingling or numbness in adults

examination.text: Supports rest or asymmetric tremor, stiffness, slowness, balance or gait difficulty, symmetrical postural tremor, medicine, thyroid and alcohol review, functional impact, head tremor, and clinician-led referral.
examination.rationale: Supports selected adult tremor referral features for neurology referral documentation; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports rest or asymmetric tremor, stiffness, slowness, balance or gait difficulty, symmetrical postural tremor, medicine, thyroid and alcohol review, functional impact, head tremor, and clinician-led referral.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Reason, symptoms, onset, progression, red flags, examination, tests, treatments, urgency, destination and patient discussion are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for neurology referral documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
